title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. basalticum
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. basalticum is a subspecies of the Hieracium glaucinum plant, which is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its bitter compounds and alkaloids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is thought to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting digestion. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
